title: Authentication, Resource Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Reader Privacy · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in privacy-minimized tenant authorization system and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.
Authorize application commands from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, purpose, and current state rather than route alone.
Require tenant scope in repository methods, SQL, keys, constraints, caches, tests, metrics, exports, support, and admin operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Token Verification
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript team handles case 2606 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove token verification, resource policy, tenant repositories, data minimization, and audit redaction; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.

Inspect the Feed Ts Token Verification trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Token Verification.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Ts Token Verification, keep Feed Ts Token Verification connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: feed-ts-security-audit-redaction-gufn7o" that uses case 3935 to prove token verification, resource policy, tenant repositories, data minimization, and audit redaction. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, leave timers, handles, or Promise rejections, or claim native runtime, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with TypeScript 7.0.2, the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility surface, Node 24.18.0, Express 5.2.1, pg 8.22.0, fast-xml-parser 5.10.0, Zod 4.4.3, PostgreSQL 18.4, original unknown fixtures, controlled Fetch and stream adapters, emitted-runtime and subprocess tests, disposable databases, handle inspection, package and image review, accessibility, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
